title: equisetum laevigatum f. polystachum
intro: Equisetum laevigatum f. polystachum, also known as the rough horsetail, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family, which is a group of ancient plants that have been around for over 400 million years. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Equisetum laevigatum f. polystachum include its ability to help with urinary issues, such as kidney stones and bladder problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
